Block.Para: An Act to amend the Employment Insurance Act (benefit period increase for regional rate of unemployment)

This bill was introduced during the First Session of the 40th Parliament. Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the House of Commons, it is deemed to have been considered and approved at all stages completed at the time of prorogation of the Second Session. The number of the bill remains unchanged.

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
This enactment increases benefit periods under the Employment Insurance Act based on regional rates of unemployment.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Employment Insurance (benefit period increase for regional rate of unemployment)

Block.Para: An Act to amend the Employment Insurance Act (benefit period increase for regional rate of unemployment)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. Section 10 of the Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Para.change: (2.1) The benefit period established by subsection (2) is increased by
(a) 2 weeks for every percentage point by which the regional rate of unemployment exceeds 4% but does not exceed 10%, and
(b) 3 weeks for every percentage point by which the regional rate of unemployment exceeds 10%,
subject to a maximum period of 52 weeks.

Block.Para:
Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
